title: Aluminum Drops as Trump Moves to Narrow Levies on Metal Products
date: Within the past 24 hours (exact date not shown)
summary: Aluminum prices fell as traders reacted to reports that President Donald Trump may narrow tariffs on some aluminum and steel products. The move raised expectations that some import levies could be rolled back, pressuring metal prices and weighing on broader industrial metals.

title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Trading Economics reported that aluminum rose to 3,596 USD/T on May 19, up 0.96% from the previous day. The page also notes a strong monthly and yearly gain, highlighting continued momentum in aluminum prices.

title: Aluminium Price Today
date: Current as of the page view
summary: Investing.com shows aluminum futures trading at 3,612.43, slightly above the previous close of 3,596.00. This suggests prices remained elevated even after the prior day’s move higher.

title: HRC Steel Price Climbs to Highest Since December 2023
summary: Hot-rolled coil steel prices rose to 1,135 USD/T on May 19, marking a 0.18% increase from the previous day and reaching the highest level since December 2023. The data also shows steel prices have gained over the past month, reflecting continued upward momentum in the market.

title: Steel Price News: Are Prices About to Drop?
summary: Ryerson reports that steel prices continue moving higher across flat, plate, and long products. The article attributes the trend to constrained supply and improving demand, suggesting market conditions remain supportive of elevated pricing.

title: Steel Prices Continue to Rise Amid Limited Spot Availability
summary: Steel Market Update says sheet prices are still rising in a market defined by extremely limited spot availability, solid demand, and long lead times. The publication also notes that sheet inventories are at very low levels, reinforcing the tight market backdrop.

title: Current State of the U.S. Steel Market
summary: This market update says U.S. steel capacity utilization has slipped below the administration's 80% target, though some short-term indicators suggest stabilization. It notes production and utilization improved in the week of May 10, 2025, and that the outlook turns more positive in 2026 as demand recovers.

title: Overcapacity will continue to weigh on steel industry
summary: The article says global steel overcapacity is continuing to pressure prices and steelmaker operations. It frames overcapacity as an ongoing drag on the industry as 2024 ends.

title: Latest developments in steelmaking capacity and outlook until 2026
summary: The OECD report says global steelmaking capacity remains far above demand and that excess capacity is a structural problem for the industry. It also projects significant additional capacity additions through 2026, with more projects underway or planned.

title: Industry Data
summary: AISI reports that domestic raw steel production in the U.S. for the week ending May 16, 2026 was 1,898,000 net tons, with capability utilization at 82.2 percent. This indicates stronger recent mill operating rates.

title: Copper - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Copper futures were reported lower, falling to around $6.1 per pound as a broader selloff in equities weighed on industrial metals. The update highlights near-term pressure on copper prices amid risk-off market sentiment.

title: Copper Futures Price Today
summary: Investing.com lists copper futures at 6.1780, slightly below the previous close of 6.1945. The page provides live pricing and market context for traders tracking short-term copper movement.

title: Copper Price (Monthly) - Historical Data & Trends
summary: YCharts shows copper at 12,950.96, up from 12,528.71 last month and 9,176.80 one year ago. This indicates a strong longer-term upward trend in copper pricing, though it is not a news article.

title: Record Margins, Shrinking Supply: Why Capital Is Chasing Copper Right Now
summary: This article discusses how strong margins and tightening copper supply are drawing investor capital back into the sector. It highlights recent production updates from copper miners, including Capstone Copper's quarterly output and Taseko Mines' first cathode production from its Florence Copper project in Arizona, a notable new U.S. greenfield copper source.

title: Copper Rebounds From 6-Week Lows After Outages At Major Mines, Limited New Project Development
summary: The segment covers copper prices rebounding after outages at major mines, with supply disruptions supporting the market. It also notes that slow Chinese demand has helped keep prices in check despite the supply-side constraints.

title: Copper price falls on Grasberg production resumption
summary: Copper prices moved lower as production resumed at Grasberg, easing some immediate supply concerns. The report notes that Freeport restarted supply to a domestic smelter in Indonesia, although the concentrate export ban at the mine remains in place.

title: Copper Futures News
summary: This news feed aggregates the latest copper market headlines, including supply, production, and policy developments affecting prices. One recent item cited in the feed mentions U.S. sanctions on an armed group and mining firms in Congo over illicit minerals, which could affect regional mining operations.

title: Copper's role in the energy transition grows as demand surges
summary: This article highlights how copper demand is rising alongside the energy transition, driven by electrification and clean energy infrastructure. It also notes that copper companies are responding by increasing production, prioritizing exploration, and adopting more sustainable and digital practices.

title: Copper must scale up production within sustainable limits
summary: The piece examines the challenge of expanding copper output while reducing environmental impact. It focuses on the need for the industry to balance growth with sustainability constraints as demand continues to increase.

title: Rewiring a sustainable, clean energy future with copper
summary: This article discusses copper’s importance in enabling a net-zero energy system because of its efficiency in transmitting electricity. It emphasizes copper’s role in reducing energy losses and supporting a more sustainable future.

title: Innovations to close the copper supply-demand gap
summary: This article covers the looming copper supply-demand gap and the innovations being developed to address it. It highlights technologies such as machine learning for exploration, mine sorting, biomining, and solvent-based extraction methods aimed at improving efficiency and reducing environmental impact.

title: Copper and the Future: Can New Demand Drivers Replace the Old?
summary: The article argues that copper is increasingly viewed as a critical infrastructure metal, not just an industrial input. It suggests that new demand drivers like electrification and technology will amplify existing demand rather than replace it.

title: Copper in the Age of AI: Challenges of Electrification
summary: This report looks at copper demand growth from electrification, energy transition, AI data centers, and defense modernization. It projects substantial demand increases through 2040 and identifies energy transition as the largest driver of future copper consumption.

title: Global Trade Update (May 2025): Focus on critical minerals
summary: This update notes that global copper demand is expected to grow significantly by 2040 while supply struggles to keep pace. It points to the need for major new mining capacity to satisfy future demand.

title: Rewiring a sustainable, clean energy future with copper
summary: This article emphasizes recycling, resource efficiency, and technological innovation as key to making copper more sustainable. It frames copper as essential to the clean energy transition while highlighting ways the industry is improving its footprint.

title: Copper for Tomorrow: Sustainable copper production
summary: This article describes an initiative focused on improving the copper value chain to support more sustainable production. It centers on innovation as a way to address challenges in meeting future copper demand responsibly.

title: Nickel rose to 18,957.25 USD/T on May 20, 2026, up 0.94% from the previous day
summary: Nickel prices continued to strengthen, reaching 18,957.25 USD/T and posting a modest daily gain. The market has also risen 4.45% over the past month and 21.29% over the past year, suggesting a broader upward trend in nickel pricing.

title: Nickel rose to 18,702.75 USD/T on May 20, 2026, up 0.23% from the previous day
summary: A separate Trading Economics update shows nickel trading at 18,702.75 USD/T, reflecting a smaller day-over-day increase. This indicates the market remains elevated and active, with prices holding near recent highs.

title: Nickel Prices April 2026 update: Northeast Asia at USD 18.93/kg down 8.8%
summary: IMARC's pricing report shows nickel prices easing across major regions in April 2026, with Northeast Asia, Europe, and North America all recording declines. The report suggests regional weakness in the near term despite broader market attention on nickel's longer-term outlook.

title: Nickel Market Shows Signs of Strength After Period of Volatility
summary: This market discussion highlights nickel stabilizing after a volatile period, with prices moving back into the $15,000-$15,800 per tonne range. It points to tightening supply conditions, declining inventories, and improving demand from stainless steel and nickel sulfate markets.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
date: Available in search result snippet only; not explicitly stated
summary: Indonesia has reportedly ordered PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's biggest nickel mine, to sharply reduce its ore production quota this year. Bloomberg says the quota could fall to 12 million tons from 42 million tons in 2025, as the country looks to support global nickel prices. The company says it plans to seek a revision.

title: Nickel Futures Rebound on Reports of Additional Output Reductions
summary: Nickel prices recovered after reports of further production cuts, with futures moving back above $18,800 per ton. The move reflects growing attention on supply discipline and its effect on market balances.

title: Unleashing sustainable nickel production for a net zero future
summary: This article looks at how optimizing nickel production can support a more sustainable battery industry. It emphasizes ESG integration, responsible investment, stakeholder engagement, and stronger reporting as key tools for reducing the environmental impact of nickel supply chains.

title: Q&A: Experts argue clean energy must secure a sustainable supply of nickel
summary: Experts discuss why nickel is increasingly important to the clean energy economy and the supply-chain risks that come with rising demand. The piece highlights the need for a secure and sustainable nickel supply to support batteries and other clean energy technologies.

title: 3 Questions: Can we secure a sustainable supply of nickel?
summary: MIT researchers examine nickel mining and why the metal is essential for electric vehicle batteries, solar panels, and wind turbines. The article frames sustainable supply as a major challenge for the energy transition, balancing demand growth with environmental and social concerns.

title: Prysmian shares rise as Italian cable maker seen as winner amid US copper tariffs
summary: Prysmian shares climbed after market commentary suggested the Italian cable maker could benefit from US copper tariffs. The article notes that investors viewed Prysmian as well-positioned in the cable sector amid changing trade conditions.

title: 0apt Ransomware Attack on Prysmian Group
summary: The ransomware group 0apt claimed responsibility for a cyberattack against Prysmian Group in Italy. The attackers threatened to leak telecommunications cable blueprints and undersea infrastructure data.
